text: When positioning a patient, responders should consider that:

options.text: Raising arms and legs will produce swelling.
Semi-sitting will improve blood flow to the heart.
Knees raised will reduce pressure on the abdomen.
Recovery position will often improve breathing.

explanation: Placing a patient with abdominal pain or injury in a semi-fowler's position with the knees flexed helps relax the abdominal musculature. This relaxation reduces tension and pressure on the abdominal wall, significantly increasing the patient's comfort.
